devanagari: अथाष्टादशोऽध्यायः । श्रीशुक उवाच । इत्थं विरिञ्चस्तुतकर्मवीर्यः प्रादुर्बभूवामृतभूरदित्याम् । चतुर्भुजः शङ्खगदाब्जचक्रः पिशङ्गवासा नलिना...
english_devnagari: athāṣṭādaśo śrīśuka uvāca itthaṃ viriñcastutakarmavīryaḥ prādurbabhūvāmṛtabhūradityām caturbhujaḥ śaṅkhagadābjacakraḥ piśaṅgavāsā nalināyatekṣaṇaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
The Lord whose glorious acts and powers were thus extolled, by god Brahmā and who is above birth and death manifested himself through Aditi, in a form endowed with four arms wielding (in each) a conch, a mace, a lotus and a discus, clad in yellow garment and with eyes wide like a lotus.

devanagari: श्यामावदातो झषराजकुण्डल त्विषोल्लसच्छ्रीवदनाम्बुजः पुमान् । श्रीवत्सवक्षा बलयाङ्गदोल्लसत्किरीटकाञ्चीगुणचारुनूपुरः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: śyāmāvadāto jhaṣarājakuṇḍala tviṣollasacchrīvadanāmbujaḥ pumān śrīvatsavakṣā balayāṅgadollasatkirīṭakāñcīguṇacārunūpuraḥ
translation: Bright dark-blue in complexion, and with the splendour of his lotus-like face enhanced with the lustre of his alligator-shaped ear-rings, the Supreme Person, with the Śrī- vatsa mark on his chest, was adorned with bracelets, armlets, a shining diadem, a girdle and a pair of beautiful anklets.

devanagari: मधुव्रातव्रतविघुष्टया स्वया विराजितः श्रीवनमालया हरिः । प्रजापतेर्वेश्मतमः स्वरोचिषा विनाशयन्कण्ठनिविष्टकौस्तुभः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: madhuvrātavratavighuṣṭayā svayā virājitaḥ śrīvanamālayā hariḥ prajāpaterveśmatamaḥ svarociṣā vināśayankaṇṭhaniviṣṭakaustubhaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Irradiated with his special splendid Vanamālā (wreath of forest flowers) resonant with a swarm of humming bees and with the Kaustubha gem suspended from his neck, Lord Hari dispelled with his splendour the gloom in the house of Kaśyapa, the Lord of Creation, with his effulgence.

devanagari: दिशः प्रसेदुः सलिलाशयास्तदा प्रजाः प्रहृष्टा ऋतवो गुणान्विताः । द्यौरन्तरीक्षं क्षितिरग्निजिह्वा गावो द्विजाः सञ्जहृषुर्नगाश्च ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: diśaḥ praseduḥ salilāśayāstadā prajāḥ prahṛṣṭā ṛtavo guṇānvitāḥ dyaurantarīkṣaṃ kṣitiragnijihvā gāvo dvijāḥ sañjahṛṣurnagāśca
translation: At that time, the cardinal points became clear and bright; lakes and ponds became translucent; all the creatures became jubilant; the seasons exhibited their characteristic efflorescence; joy reigned in the heaven, the mid air, the earth, gods (whose tongue is fire), kine, the three twice-born classes and mountains.

devanagari: श्रोणायां श्रवणद्वादश्यां मुहूर्तेऽभिजिति प्रभुः । सर्वे नक्षत्रताराद्याश्चक्रुस्तज्जन्म दक्षिणम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: śroṇāyāṃ śravaṇadvādaśyāṃ muhūrte sarve nakṣatratārādyāścakrustajjanma dakṣiṇam
translation: On the twelfth day of the bright half of the month of Bhādrapada when the moon was within the mansion called Śravaṇa, and the auspicious time was the Abhijit (the first aṃśa of Śravaṇa), the Lord incarnated (on the earth) and all constellations of stars (e.g. Aśvinī), planets etc. (by their auspicious influence) indicated his descent to be beneficial.

devanagari: द्वदश्यां सवितातिष्ठमध्यन्दिनगतो नृप । विजयानाम सा प्रोक्ता यस्यां जन्म विदुर्हरेः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: dvadaśyāṃ savitātiṣṭhamadhyandinagato nṛpa vijayānāma sā proktā yasyāṃ janma vidurhareḥ
translation: That twelfth day (of the bright half of Bhādrapada), when the sun attained the meridian, and the birth of Lord Hari took place, is known to people as Vijayā (the victorious).

devanagari: शङ्खदुन्दुभयो नेदुर्मृदङ्गपणवानकाः । चित्रवादित्रतूर्याणां निर्घोषस्तुमुलोऽभवत् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: śaṅkhadundubhayo nedurmṛdaṅgapaṇavānakāḥ citravāditratūryāṇāṃ nirghoṣastumulo
translation: Conchs were blown and big drums, kettle-drums, tabors and paṇavas (a kind of musical instrument) were sounded. There was the tumultuous sound of various musical instruments.

devanagari: प्रीताश्चाप्सरसोऽनृत्यन्गन्धर्वप्रवरा जगुः । तुष्टुवुर्मुनयो देवा मनवः पितरोऽग्नयः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: prītāścāpsaraso tuṣṭuvurmunayo devā manavaḥ pitaro

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ pre class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ pre class 2 verb]

[nominative plural from √ prī class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ prī class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ prī class 4 verb], [nominative plural from √ prī class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ prī class 9 verb], [accusative plural from √ prī class 9 verb]

translation: The celestial damsels being highly delighted danced and prominent Gandharvas (like Hāhā, Hūhū) sang. Sages, gods, Manus, manes and fire-gods extolled the Lord.

devanagari: सिद्धविद्याधरगणाः सकिम्पुरुषकिन्नराः । चारणा यक्षरक्षांसि सुपर्णा भुजगोत्तमाः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: siddhavidyādharagaṇāḥ sakimpuruṣakinnarāḥ cāraṇā yakṣarakṣāṃsi suparṇā bhujagottamāḥ
translation: Hosts of Siddhas and Vidyādharas, along with Kimpuruṣas and Kinnaras, Cāraṇas, Yakṣas and Rakṣas, Suparṇas (eagles), best of serpents, and the attendants of gods sang and danced. Highly extolling the Lord, they showered flowers on the hermitage of Aditi and its premises.

devanagari: गायन्तोऽतिप्रशंसन्तो नृत्यन्तो विबुधानुगाः । अदित्या आश्रमपदं कुसुमैः समवाकिरन् ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: gāyanto adityā āśramapadaṃ kusumaiḥ samavākiran

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ nṛt class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ nṛt class 4 verb]
[nominative single from √ ir class 6 verb], [vocative single from √ ir class 6 verb]

translation: Hosts of Siddhas and Vidyādharas, along with Kimpuruṣas and Kinnaras, Cāraṇas, Yakṣas and Rakṣas, Suparṇas (eagles), best of serpents, and the attendants of gods sang and danced. Highly extolling the Lord, they showered flowers on the hermitage of Aditi and its premises.

devanagari: दृष्ट्वादितिस्तं निजगर्भसम्भवं परं पुमांसं मुदमाप विस्मिता । गृहीतदेहं निजयोगमायया प्रजापतिश्चाह जयेति विस्मितः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭvāditistaṃ nijagarbhasambhavaṃ paraṃ pumāṃsaṃ mudamāpa vismitā gṛhītadehaṃ nijayogamāyayā prajāpatiścāha jayeti vismitaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[present middle first single], [imperative active second single]

translation: Beholding that Supreme Person as born from her womb, Aditi was astonished and was transported with joy. Amazed at the assumption of a human form by his yogic Māyā Power, even Kaśyapa, the Lord of Creation hailed him ‘May you be victorious’.

devanagari: यत्तद्वपुर्भाति विभूषणायुधैरव्यक्तचिद्व्यक्तमधारयद्धरिः । बभूव तेनैव स वामनो वटुः सम्पश्यतोर्दिव्यगतिर्यथा नटः ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: yattadvapurbhāti vibhūṣaṇāyudhairavyaktacidvyaktamadhārayaddhariḥ babhūva tenaiva sa vāmano vaṭuḥ sampaśyatordivyagatiryathā naṭaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active third plural], [aorist active third plural]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[genitive dual from √ śā class 4 verb], [locative dual from √ śā class 4 verb]
[genitive dual from √ śā class 4 verb], [locative dual from √ śā class 4 verb]

translation: With that very form, decorated with ornaments and endowed with weapons which Hari, of unmanifest consciousness, manifested to them, he (instantly) transformed Himself in a dwarf Brāhmaṇa boy like a wonderfully active actor, while the parents were simply looking on.

devanagari: तं वटुं वामनं दृष्ट्वा मोदमाना महर्षयः । कर्माणि कारयामासुः पुरस्कृत्य प्रजापतिम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ vaṭuṃ vāmanaṃ dṛṣṭvā modamānā maharṣayaḥ karmāṇi kārayāmāsuḥ puraskṛtya prajāpatim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ mud class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ mud class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ mud class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ mud class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ mud class 1 verb]

translation: Seeing (the transformation of the Lord as) the dwarf Brāhmaṇa boy, all great sages were delighted and with Kaśyapa, the Lord of creation, as their head, got performed all the religious rites and observances (from those beginning with the birth e.g. jātakarman and other).

devanagari: तस्योपनीयमानस्य सावित्रीं सविताब्रवीत् । बृहस्पतिर्ब्रह्मसूत्रं मेखलां कश्यपोऽददात् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyopanīyamānasya sāvitrīṃ savitābravīt bṛhaspatirbrahmasūtraṃ mekhalāṃ kaśyapo

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [genitive single from √ man class 4 verb], [genitive single from √ man class 8 verb]
[genitive single from √ man class 4 verb], [genitive single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: [1] While his sacred thread ceremony (Upanayana) was being performed, the sun-god (the presiding deity of Gāyatrī Mantra) taught him the Gāyatrī Mantra; Bṛhaspati, the preceptor of gods, gave him the sacred thread, and Kaśyapa, the cord of muñja grass to be tied round his waist.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
[verses 14-17] The list of deities who presented Vāmana different articles differs in other Purāṇas. Thus in Matsya-purāṇa 245-85 we find the black antelope skin given by Brahmā, the sacred thread by Bṛhaspati, the sacred staff by Marīci, Kamaṇḍalu by Vasiṣṭha. a bunch of Kuśa grass by Aṅgiras, rosary of beads by Pulaha, a pair of white garments by Pulastya. The four Vedas, Sāṃkhya, Yoga and other Śāstras attended upon Vāmana.

devanagari: ददौ कृष्णाजिनं भूमिर्दण्डं सोमो वनस्पतिः । कौपीनाच्छादनं माता द्यौश्छत्रं जगतः पतेः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: dadau kṛṣṇājinaṃ bhūmirdaṇḍaṃ somo vanaspatiḥ kaupīnācchādanaṃ mātā dyauśchatraṃ jagataḥ pateḥ

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: [1] The Earth-goddess conferred the skin of black-antelope on him, while Soma, the Lord of plant and herbs offered the sacred staff. The mother covered his nudity with a piece of cloth, and heavens gave an umbrella to the Lord of the Universe.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
[verses 14-17] The list of deities who presented Vāmana different articles differs in other Purāṇas. Thus in Matsya-purāṇa 245-85 we find the black antelope skin given by Brahmā, the sacred thread by Bṛhaspati, the sacred staff by Marīci, Kamaṇḍalu by Vasiṣṭha. a bunch of Kuśa grass by Aṅgiras, rosary of beads by Pulaha, a pair of white garments by Pulastya. The four Vedas, Sāṃkhya, Yoga and other Śāstras attended upon Vāmana.

devanagari: कमण्डलुं वेदगर्भः कुशान्सप्तर्षयो ददुः । अक्षमालां महाराज सरस्वत्यव्ययात्मनः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: kamaṇḍaluṃ vedagarbhaḥ kuśānsaptarṣayo daduḥ akṣamālāṃ mahārāja sarasvatyavyayātmanaḥ
translation: [1] God Brahmā presented him a water pot (of gourd or wood) and the seven sages, the darbha grass. The goddess Sarasvatī gave a rosary of beads to the Immortal Supreme Soul.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
[verses 14-17] The list of deities who presented Vāmana different articles differs in other Purāṇas. Thus in Matsya-purāṇa 245-85 we find the black antelope skin given by Brahmā, the sacred thread by Bṛhaspati, the sacred staff by Marīci, Kamaṇḍalu by Vasiṣṭha. a bunch of Kuśa grass by Aṅgiras, rosary of beads by Pulaha, a pair of white garments by Pulastya. The four Vedas, Sāṃkhya, Yoga and other Śāstras attended upon Vāmana.

devanagari: तस्मा इत्युपनीताय यक्षराट्पात्रिकामदात् । भिक्षां भगवती साक्षादुमादादम्बिका सती ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmā ityupanītāya yakṣarāṭpātrikāmadāt bhikṣāṃ bhagavatī sākṣādumādādambikā satī

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]

translation: [1] When his initiation-ceremony (sacred-thread-investiture ceremony) was over, Kubera, King of Yakṣas gave him a begging-bowl with words “This is for him”. And the veritable Mother-goddess Satī (Umā) herself served him the alms.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
[verses 14-17] The list of deities who presented Vāmana different articles differs in other Purāṇas. Thus in Matsya-purāṇa 245-85 we find the black antelope skin given by Brahmā, the sacred thread by Bṛhaspati, the sacred staff by Marīci, Kamaṇḍalu by Vasiṣṭha. a bunch of Kuśa grass by Aṅgiras, rosary of beads by Pulaha, a pair of white garments by Pulastya. The four Vedas, Sāṃkhya, Yoga and other Śāstras attended upon Vāmana.

devanagari: स ब्रह्मवर्चसेनैवं सभां सम्भावितो वटुः । ब्रह्मर्षिगणसञ्जुष्टामत्यरोचत मारिषः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: sa brahmavarcasenaivaṃ sabhāṃ sambhāvito vaṭuḥ brahmarṣigaṇasañjuṣṭāmatyarocata māriṣaḥ
translation: Thus honoured by all, the newly initiated eminent Brāhmaṇa boy, with his splendid Brahmanic (divine) effulgence, surpassed the whole assembly of Brāhmaṇa-sages.

devanagari: समिद्धमाहितं वह्निं कृत्वा परिसमूहनम् । परिस्तीर्य समभ्यर्च्य समिद्भिरजुहोद्द्विजः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: samiddhamāhitaṃ vahniṃ kṛtvā parisamūhanam paristīrya samabhyarcya samidbhirajuhoddvijaḥ
translation: The twice-born boy instituted the sacred fire (on the altar) enkindling it, duly worshipping it by placing the Kuśa grass around it in their proper places (paristīrya), and oblated into the fire the sacred sticks (daubed with ghee—the samidhs).

devanagari: श्रुत्वाश्वमेधैर्यजमानमूर्जितं बलिं भृगूणामुपकल्पितैस्ततः । जगाम तत्रालिसारसम्भृतो भारेण गां सन्नमयन्पदे पदे ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: śrutvāśvamedhairyajamānamūrjitaṃ baliṃ bhṛgūṇāmupakalpitaistataḥ jagāma tatrālisārasambhṛto bhāreṇa gāṃ sannamayanpade pade

[nominative single from √ sad class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ sad class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ sad class 6 verb], [accusative single from √ sad class 6 verb]

translation: Having learnt that Bali, of enormous wealth and power, was engaged as a sacrificer in a horse-sacrifice conducted by the Brāhmaṇas of the Bhṛgu clan, he (Vāmana) concentrating in himself all the power, proceeded (from the hermitage of Aditi) to that place (of Bali’s sacrificial hall), bending down the earth at every step, on account of his weight.

devanagari: तं नर्मदायास्तट उत्तरे बलेर्य ऋत्विजस्ते भृगुकच्छसंज्ञके । प्रवर्तयन्तो भृगवः क्रतूत्तमं व्यचक्षतारादुदितं यथा रविम् ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ narmadāyāstaṭa uttare balerya ṛtvijaste bhṛgukacchasaṃjñake pravartayanto bhṛgavaḥ kratūttamaṃ vyacakṣatārāduditaṃ yathā ravim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative plural from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ vad class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ vad class 1 verb]

translation: The sacrificial priests of the Bhṛgu clan who were conducting the best of sacrifices (viz. a horse-sacrifice) of Bali on the northern bank of the Narmadā, at a spot called Bhṛgu- Kacchaka (near modern Broach in Gujarat), beheld him (Vāmana) approaching them, brilliant like the sun risen just near them.

devanagari: ते ऋत्विजो यजमानः सदस्या हतत्विषो वामनतेजसा नृप । सूर्यः किलायात्युत वा विभावसुः सनत्कुमारोऽथ दिदृक्षया क्रतोः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: te ṛtvijo yajamānaḥ sadasyā hatatviṣo vāmanatejasā nṛpa sūryaḥ kilāyātyuta vā vibhāvasuḥ sanatkumāro

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ sūr class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ sūr class 10 verb]
[genitive dual from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [locative dual from √ kṛ class 2 verb]
[genitive dual from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [locative dual from √ kṛ class 2 verb]

translation: Oh King, their glory being eclipsed by the effulgence of Vāmana, the priests conducting the sacrifice, the sacrificer Bali and the members of the sacrificial assembly began to conjecture (among themselves) whether he (Vāmana) was the Sun-god himself or was the Fire-god or the (great) sage Sanatkumāra himself, coming with a view to observe the sacrifice.

devanagari: इत्थं सशिष्येषु भृगुष्वनेकधा वितर्क्यमाणो भगवान्स वामनः । छत्रं सदण्डं सजलं कमण्डलुं विवेश बिभ्रद्धयमेधवाटम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: itthaṃ saśiṣyeṣu bhṛguṣvanekadhā vitarkyamāṇo bhagavānsa vāmanaḥ chatraṃ sadaṇḍaṃ sajalaṃ kamaṇḍaluṃ viveśa bibhraddhayamedhavāṭam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: While the Bhṛgus along with their disciples were thus variously expressing their surmise about him, Lord Vāmana carrying with him his umbrella, sacred staff, and water-bowl full of water, entered the panda! of the horsesacrifice.

devanagari: मौञ्ज्या मेखलया वीतमुपवीताजिनोत्तरम् । जटिलं वामनं विप्रं मायामाणवकं हरिम् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: mauñjyā mekhalayā vītamupavītājinottaram jaṭilaṃ vāmanaṃ vipraṃ māyāmāṇavakaṃ harim

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ vai class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ vai class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ vī class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ vī class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ vī class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ vī class 4 verb]
[nominative single from √ vyā class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ vyā class 1 verb]

translation: Noticing the entry of Lord Hari, a dwarfish Brāhmaṇa boy with the (outward appearance of a Brahmacārin assumed with his Māyā powers, having the zone of Muñja-grass tied round his waist, and wearing as an upper garment a deer-skin in the fashion of the sacred thread (over his shoulder) and matted hair, the Bhṛgus along with their disciples and fires (fire-gods) as well, shrank before his divine lustre, and standing up to honour him, accorded unto him reception with all the due formalities.

devanagari: प्रविष्टं वीक्ष्य भृगवः सशिष्यास्ते सहाग्निभिः । प्रत्यगृह्णन्समुत्थाय सङ्क्षिप्तास्तस्य तेजसा ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: praviṣṭaṃ vīkṣya bhṛgavaḥ saśiṣyāste sahāgnibhiḥ pratyagṛhṇansamutthāya saṅkṣiptāstasya tejasā
translation: Noticing the entry of Lord Hari, a dwarfish Brāhmaṇa boy with the (outward appearance of a Brahmacārin assumed with his Māyā powers, having the zone of Muñja-grass tied round his waist, and wearing as an upper garment a deer-skin in the fashion of the sacred thread (over his shoulder) and matted hair, the Bhṛgus along with their disciples and fires (fire-gods) as well, shrank before his divine lustre, and standing up to honour him, accorded unto him reception with all the due formalities.

devanagari: यजमानः प्रमुदितो दर्शनीयं मनोरमम् । रूपानुरूपावयवं तस्मा आसनमाहरत् ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: yajamānaḥ pramudito darśanīyaṃ manoramam rūpānurūpāvayavaṃ tasmā āsanamāharat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ dṛś class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ dṛś ]
[nominative single from √ dṛś class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ dṛś class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ dṛś ], [accusative single from √ dṛś ]

translation: Overjoyed to see his charming personality worth seeing, with limbs proportionate to his form, the sacrificer himself offered him a suitable seat.

devanagari: स्वागतेनाभिनन्द्याथ पादौ भगवतो बलिः । अवनिज्यार्चयामास मुक्तसङ्गमनोरमम् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: svāgatenābhinandyātha pādau bhagavato baliḥ avanijyārcayāmāsa muktasaṅgamanoramam
translation: According him courteous welcome, Bali washed the feet of the Lord, and worshipped him who is attractive to the minds of those who have abjured all attachment.

devanagari: तत्पादशौचं जनकल्मषापहं स धर्मविन्मूर्त्यदधात्सुमङ्गलम् । यद्देवदेवो गिरिशश्चन्द्र मौलिर्दधार मूर्ध्ना परया च भक्त्या ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: tatpādaśaucaṃ janakalmaṣāpahaṃ sa dharmavinmūrtyadadhātsumaṅgalam yaddevadevo giriśaścandra maulirdadhāra mūrdhnā parayā ca bhaktyā

translation: Bali, the knower of the path of righteousness, placed (sprinkled) on his head the water with which he washed his feet, and hence had become most auspicious and capable of removing all the sins of the people, and which Lord Śiva (the Lord of mount Kailāśa) the god of gods and the wearer of the crescent of the moon on his forehead, received on his head with utmost devotion.

devanagari: श्रीबलिरुवाच । स्वागतं ते नमस्तुभ्यं ब्रह्मन्किं करवाम ते । ब्रह्मर्षीणां तपः साक्षान्मन्ये त्वार्य वपुर्धरम् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: śrībaliruvāca svāgataṃ te namastubhyaṃ brahmankiṃ karavāma te brahmarṣīṇāṃ tapaḥ sākṣānmanye tvārya vapurdharam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[present middle first single], [present passive first single]

translation:
Bali said:
(Hearty) welcome to you. I pay my obeisance to you. Oh Brahman! (Be pleased to tell us) what we should do for you. Oh noble Sir, I deem that you are the very embodiment of the penance of all Brāhmaṇa sages.

devanagari: अद्य नः पितरस्तृप्ता अद्य नः पावितं कुलम् । अद्य स्विष्टः क्रतुरयं यद्भवानागतो गृहान् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: adya naḥ pitarastṛptā adya naḥ pāvitaṃ kulam adya sviṣṭaḥ kraturayaṃ yadbhavānāgato gṛhān

translation: (Fully) satisfied are all our fore-fathers today. Our family and house have been purified. This very sacrifice has been successfully performed inasmuch as your worship has visited our house.

devanagari: अद्याग्नयो मे सुहुता यथाविधि द्विजात्मज त्वच्चरणावनेजनैः । हतांहसो वार्भिरियं च भूरहो तथा पुनीता तनुभिः पदैस्तव ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: adyāgnayo me suhutā yathāvidhi dvijātmaja tvaccaraṇāvanejanaiḥ hatāṃhaso vārbhiriyaṃ ca bhūraho tathā punītā tanubhiḥ padaistava

translation: The sacrificial fires of mine whose sins have been completely annihilated by the waters used for washing your feet, stand properly worshipped with oblations, according to sacriptural injunctions. This land of our has been purified by your tiny feet, Oh son of a Brāhmaṇa!

devanagari: यद्यद्वटो वाञ्छसि तत्प्रतीच्छ मे त्वामर्थिनं विप्रसुतानुतर्कये । गां काञ्चनं गुणवद्धाम मृष्टं तथान्नपेयमुत वा विप्रकन्याम् । ग्रामान्सम...
english_devnagari: yadyadvaṭo vāñchasi tatpratīccha me tvāmarthinaṃ viprasutānutarkaye gāṃ kāñcanaṃ guṇavaddhāma mṛṣṭaṃ tathānnapeyamuta vā viprakanyām grāmānsamṛddhāṃsturagāngajānvā rathāṃstathārhattama sampratīccha

translation:
Oh son of a Brāhmaṇa! Methinks you have come to seek something. Be pleased to accept from me whatever you desire, Oh Brahmacārin! Oh most worshipful Brāhmaṇa! Duly accept from me either the earth, gold, a well-furnished house, delicious food and drink or possibly even a girl or prosperous villages, horses, elephants or chariots, Oh Brāhmaṇa.
